Title: LATERAL BRANCHING OXIDOREDUCTASE, one novel target gene of Squamosa Promoter Binding Protein-like 2, regulates tillering in switchgrass

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts in SL signaling and interacts with PvSPL2, linking signaling to transcriptional control of tillering
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; LUC reporter assay (interaction context discussed)
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvD53 physically interacts with PvSPL2. 2. This interaction supports crosstalk between SL signaling and the miR156-SPL2 module during tillering regulation. 3. The paper proposes that increased SLs promote D53 degradation, releasing PvSPL2 activity.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not the main metabolic enzyme or TF target, but experimentally implicated as a signaling component in the core regulatory model.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential signaling-node target for architecture engineering, though not directly manipulated here

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Irregular relationship with PvLBO in SPL4OE lines
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Has potential to activate PvLBO promoter in assays, but no consistent in vivo effect on PvLBO expression was detected
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; dual-LUC assay; expression analysis in SPL4OE lines
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvSPL4 can bind the PvLBO promoter and activate reporter expression in transient assays. 2. However, PvLBO expression changes were irregular in SPL4OE switchgrass, indicating a complicated or context-dependent relationship. 3. PvSPL4 was not supported as the main in vivo regulator of PvLBO in this paper.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a secondary experimentally tested factor, but evidence is weaker than for PvSPL2.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Uncertain because of inconsistent in vivo behavior

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely oxidizes methyl carlactonoate (MeCLA) to 1′-HO-MeCLA in a late strigolactone biosynthetic step
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: noncanonical strigolactone biosynthetic late step after CLA synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: downstream late-acting step after MAX1/CLA formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms late bioactive strigolactone precursor/product that promotes active SL production
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: supported as a key control enzyme in late SL biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Ubiquitous; highest in E2L2 leaf tissue, followed by E3L2, then crown buds and roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Ubiquitous; highest in E2L2 leaf tissue, followed by E3L2, then crown buds and roots; preferentially expressed at the apex of shoot apical and floral meristems
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant hormone controlling tiller/branch outgrowth and plant architecture
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of PvLBO reduced tiller number, whereas RNAi downregulation increased tiller number and promoted axillary bud outgrowth; overexpression also decreased carotenoid and chlorophyll contents and reduced acid-insoluble lignin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression; RNAi knockdown; rescue in miR156OE background; rac-GR24 and TIS108 pharmacological treatments; qRT-PCR; RNA-seq; in situ hybridization
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Glasshouse growth; rac-GR24 treatment; TIS108 treatment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvLBO transcripts were reduced in miR156-overexpressing switchgrass and negatively correlated with miR156 abundance. 2. PvLBO overexpression suppressed tillering in wild-type and partially counteracted the high-tillering phenotype of miR156OE plants. 3. PvLBO RNAi increased tillering and axillary bud outgrowth, supporting a positive role in strigolactone biosynthesis. 4. PvLBO overexpression caused feedback repression of SL biosynthetic genes and induction of SL signaling genes, consistent with elevated bioactive SL status.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors infer function from homology to AtLBO and transcriptional/phenotypic evidence; direct metabolite measurement of SL products was not reported and future SL measurements/feeding/heterologous enzyme assays were proposed.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to tune tiller number, plant architecture, and biomass yield in forage/bioenergy switchgrass, but tradeoffs with semidwarfing, carotenoid/chlorophyll reduction, and lignin changes should be considered
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression reduced tiller number and lignin and lowered carotenoid/chlorophyll; RNAi caused semidwarf phenotype with shorter internodes

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Squamosa Promoter Binding Protein-like (SBP/SPL) transcription factor
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly activates PvLBO transcription by binding GTAC motifs in its promoter; upregulates PvLBO, PvMAX2, PvD14, and PvTB1 in SPL2OE plants
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation; protein interaction with signaling repressor D53
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Acts as a key upstream activator linking the miR156-SPL module to strigolactone biosynthesis via PvLBO
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: miR156; strigolactone signaling via D53 interaction
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Proposed feedforward loop: PvSPL2 activates PvLBO to increase SLs, and increased SL signaling promotes D53 degradation, releasing more PvSPL2 activity
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed with PvLBO in roots, crown and nodes at E4 stage
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Co-expressed with PvLBO in roots, crown and nodes at E4 stage
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes expression of PvLBO and SL signaling genes, thereby contributing to reduced tillering
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: co-expression analysis; dual-LUC assay; ChIP-qPCR; Y1H; EMSA; overexpression analysis; qRT-PCR; in situ hybridization; Y2H
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvSPL2 directly binds GTAC motifs in the PvLBO promoter and activates its transcription. 2. PvLBO transcript abundance increases in PvSPL2 overexpression plants and decreases in miR156OE plants. 3. PvSPL2 interacts with PvD53, linking SPL-mediated transcription with SL signaling. 4. PvSPL2 is a central node coupling the miR156 pathway to SL biosynthesis and tillering control.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Expression atlas probe sets cited: PvSPL2 AP13CTG29191_at; study RNA-seq context accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper positions PvSPL2, not PvSPL4, as the main experimentally supported regulator of PvLBO in vivo.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful regulatory target for engineering tiller number and architecture through modulation of SL biosynthesis in switchgrass
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely affects broader developmental traits controlled by miR156-SPL module
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter in transient assay; pANIC6B in overexpression constructs

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: indirect control of strigolactone biosynthesis through SPL repression
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Negatively regulates PvSPL2, leading to reduced PvLBO expression and impaired SL biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: High miR156 lowers PvLBO transcript abundance indirectly and is associated with impaired SL biosynthesis and increased tillering
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Mature miR156 abundance negatively correlated with PvLBO expression across miR156OE lines
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpressed transgenic lines grouped by increasing miR156 levels
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increased tillering and reduced PvLBO expression; PvLBO overexpression partially reversed the miR156OE tillering phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic overexpression; qRT-PCR; correlation analysis; pharmacological treatment with rac-GR24/TIS108; genetic rescue with PvLBO overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. miR156 overexpression is associated with reduced PvLBO transcript levels and increased tillering. 2. The miR156OE phenotype responds to rac-GR24 but not TIS108 as wild type does, suggesting impaired downstream SL biosynthesis. 3. PvLBO overexpression in miR156OE background reduces tiller number, indicating PvLBO acts downstream of the miR156-SPL module.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the study explicitly tests the miR156-SPL-PvLBO regulatory module affecting SL-associated tillering.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A powerful upstream lever for architecture manipulation, though pleiotropic effects are expected
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad pleiotropic developmental effects due to miR156 control of multiple SPL targets
